definitions.definition: The process of instructing, guiding, counselling, motivating, and leading people in the organisation to achieve its objectives. It is the heart of management.
Overseeing the work of subordinates to ensure tasks are performed as planned. The supervisor is the link between workers and management.
The process of stimulating people to act willingly towards organisational goals by satisfying their needs and wants.
The ability to influence others to work willingly toward group objectives. A leader guides, inspires, and directs.
The process of exchanging information, ideas, and understanding between a sender and receiver through a chosen channel.
Five levels of human needs: Physiological → Safety → Social → Esteem → Self-Actualisation. A satisfied need no longer motivates.
Basic survival needs — food, shelter, clothing. In organisation: basic salary and wages.
Protection from danger and stability. In organisation: job security, pension plan, insurance.
Friendship, acceptance, belonging. In organisation: cordial relations with colleagues, team activities.
Status, recognition, respect, self-confidence. In organisation: job title, promotions, awards.
Realising full potential, personal growth, achieving the impossible. In organisation: challenging work, creative opportunities.
Leader takes all decisions without consulting subordinates. Centralised authority. Quick but leads to low morale.
Leader consults subordinates before making decisions. Shared authority. Higher morale and commitment.
Leader gives full freedom to subordinates to make decisions. Minimal supervision. Works only with highly skilled teams.
Informal communication network arising from social interactions. Faster than formal channels but may carry rumours.
Communication barriers caused by words interpreted differently, technical jargon, or body language mismatch.

distinctions.between: Autocratic vs Democratic vs Laissez-faire Leadership

points.b: Shared with team / Fully with subordinates

scenarios.scenario: A manager gives full freedom to team leaders to plan and execute their projects independently without any interference.
Before making a decision about the new product launch, the CEO holds a meeting with all department heads to hear their views.
During a factory fire, the plant manager immediately takes charge and gives orders without consulting anyone.
scenarios.principle: Leader decides alone — suitable for emergencies

scenarios.scenario: An employee receives a basic salary to meet food, shelter, and clothing needs.
scenarios.principle: Basic survival needs — lowest level of hierarchy

scenarios.scenario: A company offers pension plans and job security to its employees.
The company organises team lunches and recreational activities for employees.
A manager is given a prestigious job title and publicly recognised for outstanding performance.
An engineer is given a challenging R&D project with full creative freedom to innovate.
scenarios.principle: Realising full potential through challenging, creative work

scenarios.scenario: A technical report uses complex jargon that the production workers cannot understand.
A company offers its employees stock options and profit-sharing bonuses.
